cellestial.layers.outline
=========================

.. py:module:: cellestial.layers.outline


Functions
---------

.. autoapisummary::

   cellestial.layers.outline.cluster_outlines


Module Contents
---------------

.. py:function:: cluster_outlines(plot: lets_plot.plot.core.PlotSpec, /, groups: str | collections.abc.Sequence[str | collections.abc.Sequence[str]], *, padding: float = 1.5, level: float = 0.04, grid_size: int = 200, color: str = '#1f1f1f', linetype: Literal['blank', 'solid', 'dashed', 'dotted', 'dotdash', 'longdash', 'twodash'] = 'dashed', mapping: lets_plot.plot.core.FeatureSpec | None = None, size: float = 1, group_by: str | None = None, x: str | None = None, y: str | None = None, **geom_kwargs) -> lets_plot.plot.core.LayerSpec

   Returns a Layer of `geom_path` that outlines the given clusters.

   :param plot: The plot to which the layer will be added. Used to extract data and aesthetics.
   :type plot: PlotSpec
   :param groups: The group(s) to outline. Can be a single group name or a list of group
                  Providing string(s) will outline clusters with those group names.
                  Providing nested sequences of strings combines each sequence into a their own group.
                  E.g `groups=[["A", "B"]]` will outline groups A and B as if they were one cluster.
   :type groups: str | Sequence[str | Sequence[str]]
   :param padding: The spatial buffer added to the cluster's bounding box before calculating density.
                   Increasing this value allows the density 'cloud' to expand further from the outermost
                   points, ensuring the resulting curve is smooth and not truncated at the data edges.
   :type padding: float, default=1.5
   :param level: The density threshold for the outline, expressed as a fraction of the peak density
                 (0.0 to 1.0). A lower value creates a larger, more inclusive 'bubble' that captures
                 outliers, while a higher value creates a tighter boundary around the cluster's core.
   :type level: float, default=0.04
   :param grid_size: The resolution of the internal sampling grid used for the Marching Squares algorithm.
                     A higher value produces a smoother, more 'curvy' path by increasing the number of
                     interpolation points, while a lower value improves calculation speed but may
                     result in a more jagged or 'pixelated' appearance.
   :type grid_size: int, default=200
   :param color: The color of the outline.
   :type color: str, default="#1f1f1f"
   :param linetype: The linetype of the outline. E.g "dashed", "dotted",
   :type linetype: str, default="dashed"
   :param mapping: Additional aesthetic mappings for the plot, the result of `aes()`.
   :type mapping: FeatureSpec | None, default=None
   :param size: The size of the outline.
   :type size: float, default=1
   :param group_by: The column name in the data used to group clusters by. e.g "cell_type".
                    If None, it will be inferred from the plot aesthetics.
   :type group_by: str | None, default=None
   :param x: The column name in the data used for x-axis coordinates. e.g "X_UMAP1".
             If None, it will be inferred from the plot aesthetics.
   :type x: str | None, default=None
   :param y: The column name in the data used for y-axis coordinates. e.g "X_UMAP2".
             If None, it will be inferred from the plot aesthetics.
   :type y: str | None, default=None
   :param \*\*geom_kwargs: Additional parameters for the `geom_path` layer.
                           For more information on geom_path parameters, see:
                           https://lets-plot.org/python/pages/api/lets_plot.geom_path.html
